narrative.headline: 2020 Toyota Highlander: the power train dominates owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 290 complaints with NHTSA. The power train is by far the most reported area at 39%, more than triple the next areas: service brakes (11%) and air bags (11%). No fires and no deaths are reported across any area. The air bags draw the most injuries (5) of any single area, so they are worth a close look.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint by a wide margin: 114 reports (39%), tied to 2 crashes and 1 injury. No fires reported here.

issues.sample: I HAVE BEEN HAVING INTERMITTENT ISSUES WITH THE BRAKES WHEN TURNING ON MY CAR AND GOING IN REVERSE. ON ONE OCCASION, AFTER TURNING ON THE VEHICLE AND PUTTING IT IN REVERSE, THE VEHICLE DID NOT MOVE DESPITE BEING ON LEVEL GROUND. I HAD TO APPLY PRESSURE ON THE ACCELERATOR AND HEAR
issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 33 reports (11%), linked to 3 crashes and 1 injury. Test the brakes carefully, including in reverse.

issues.sample: Air bags didn’t deploy in severe accident.  Vehicle is available for inspection.  Air bags not deploying in accident could have led to injuries.  No service or dealerships has seen the vehicle.  Police didn’t inspect the vehicle.  Insurance company is going to look at the vehicle
issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: 32 reports (11%), but the most injuries of any area: 5 injuries plus 3 crashes. Worth checking closely given the outcomes.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: 27 reports (9%), with 1 crash and no reported injuries or fires. A smaller but recurring complaint area.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: The fewest of the top five: 17 reports (6%), tied to 1 crash and 2 injuries.
